lessons.title: Learn: Shell Scripting Basics
lessons.content_md:
# Lab 09: Shell Scripting Basics

Shell scripting helps automate repeated command-line tasks. A script is just a text file with shell commands executed in sequence.

## Learning Objectives

- Create and run Bash scripts safely
- Use variables and command substitution
- Write basic conditionals and loops
- Handle script arguments and exit codes
- Save output to files for automation logs

## Core Topics

### 1) Script Structure

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
```

### 2) Variables and Output

```bash
name=student
echo "Hello $name"
```

### 3) Conditions

```bash
if [[ -f file.txt ]]; then
  echo "exists"
fi
```

### 4) Loops

```bash
for n in 1 2 3; do
  echo "$n"
done
```

### 5) Safety Rules

- Always quote variables when expanding paths
- Use `set -euo pipefail` in automation scripts
- Check script permissions before execution
- Print clear markers for validation and logs

After Learn, complete command practice, then exercises, then MCQ.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 1 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 1

Create script files and run basic Bash logic.

```bash
cd ~ && mkdir -p ~/lab9/scripts
cat > ~/lab9/scripts/hello.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
echo HELLO_LAB9
EOF
chmod +x ~/lab9/scripts/hello.sh
~/lab9/scripts/hello.sh
name='hacker' && echo "NAME_IS_${name^^}"
current_dir=$(pwd) && echo "DIR_CAPTURED:${current_dir##*/}"
cat > ~/lab9/scripts/check_file.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
if [[ -f /etc/passwd ]]; then echo PASSWD_EXISTS; else echo PASSWD_MISSING; fi
EOF
chmod +x ~/lab9/scripts/check_file.sh && ~/lab9/scripts/check_file.sh
for n in 1 2 3; do echo LOOP_$n; done
printf '%s
' alpha beta gamma > ~/lab9/words.txt && wc -l ~/lab9/words.txt
```

tasks.description: C01: Create scripts directory for lab9.
C02: Create hello.sh that prints HELLO_LAB9.
C03: Make hello.sh executable.
C04: Execute hello.sh and show HELLO_LAB9 output.
C05: Set a variable and print uppercase marker NAME_IS_HACKER.
C06: Capture current folder name and print DIR_CAPTURED marker.
C07: Create and run check_file.sh to print PASSWD_EXISTS.
tasks.hint: Script should test /etc/passwd with if condition.

tasks.description: C08: Print LOOP_1 LOOP_2 LOOP_3 with a for loop.
tasks.hint: Type: for n in 1 2 3; do echo LOOP_$n; done

tasks.description: C09: Create words.txt with 3 lines: alpha, beta, gamma.
tasks.hint: Type: printf '%s\n' alpha beta gamma > ~/lab9/words.txt && ls ~/lab9

tasks.description: C10: Show line count of words.txt.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 2 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 2

Use arguments, functions, case, and output redirection.

```bash
cat > ~/lab9/scripts/arg_printer.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
echo ARG1:$1
EOF
chmod +x ~/lab9/scripts/arg_printer.sh && ~/lab9/scripts/arg_printer.sh test123
cat > ~/lab9/scripts/sum_loop.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
sum=0
for n in 1 2 3 4 5; do sum=$((sum+n)); done
echo SUM:$sum
EOF
chmod +x ~/lab9/scripts/sum_loop.sh && ~/lab9/scripts/sum_loop.sh
cat > ~/lab9/scripts/case_demo.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
case "$1" in
  start) echo ACTION_START ;;
  stop) echo ACTION_STOP ;;
  *) echo ACTION_UNKNOWN ;;
esac
EOF
chmod +x ~/lab9/scripts/case_demo.sh && ~/lab9/scripts/case_demo.sh start
~/lab9/scripts/case_demo.sh stop
~/lab9/scripts/case_demo.sh restart
~/lab9/scripts/sum_loop.sh > ~/lab9/sum.log && cat ~/lab9/sum.log
grep 'SUM:' ~/lab9/sum.log && echo SUM_LOG_OK
```

tasks.description: C11: Create arg_printer.sh that prints ARG1:<value>.
C12: Create sum_loop.sh and print SUM:15.
tasks.hint: Use for loop from 1 to 5 with arithmetic expansion.

tasks.description: C13: Create case_demo.sh and run with start.
tasks.hint: Use case statement and run: ~/lab9/scripts/case_demo.sh start

tasks.description: C14: Run case_demo.sh with stop argument.
C15: Run case_demo.sh with unknown argument.
C16: Save sum_loop.sh output to sum.log.
C17: Show content of sum.log.
C18: Validate SUM marker in sum.log using grep.
C19: Print marker SUM_LOG_OK after grep validation.
C20: List scripts folder content and include case_demo.sh.

lessons.title: Exercises (5)
lessons.content_md:
## Practical Exercises

Complete all exercises to prove scripting basics.

- E1-E2: easy
- E3-E4: medium
- E5: medium-hard

tasks.description: E1: Create env_report.sh that prints USER and HOME with prefix ENV_REPORT.
E2: Run env_report.sh and redirect output to env.log, then print ENV_LOG_DONE.
E3: Create count_to_n.sh that loops from 1 to 5 and prints COUNT_5 at end.
tasks.hint: Use a for loop and run the script.

tasks.description: E4: Create file_check.sh that prints FOUND_WORDS when ~/lab9/words.txt exists.
E5: Create cleanup.sh to remove ~/lab9/temp.tmp and print CLEANUP_OK.
tasks.hint: Create temp.tmp first, remove inside script, then echo marker.

lessons.title: MCQ Quiz (5)
lessons.content_md:
## MCQ: Shell Scripting Basics

Answer using option index (0-3). Example: submit 2.

tasks.description: Q1: Which line is commonly used as a Bash shebang?
Q2: Which syntax accesses the first script argument?
Q3: Which statement is used for multi-branch matching in Bash?
Q4: What does chmod +x script.sh enable?
tasks.validation_rule: ["Deletes the script","Compresses the script","Moves the script","Makes the script executable"]

tasks.description: Q5: Why use set -euo pipefail in scripts?
tasks.validation_rule: ["To hide all errors","To run script in background","To fail fast on errors and unset vars","To disable variable expansion"]
